conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a semester-long plan for conducting a study on the impact of social media on mental health among teenagers.
org_constraint: Ensure the phrase "mental health challenges" is included in the plan.
new_constraint: The word "challenge" can only appear in the singular form in the plan.
instruction: Write a semester-long plan for conducting a study on the impact of social media on mental health among teenagers. Ensure the phrase "mental health challenges" is included in the plan. The word "challenge" can only appear in the singular form in the plan.
response:
# Semester-Long Study Plan: Social Media and Adolescent Well-being

## Month 1: Conceptualization and Literature Review
The first month will focus on defining the scope of research and reviewing existing academic literature regarding how digital platforms influence adolescent psychology. We will identify specific indicators of well-being to track and formulate a clear hypothesis linking screen time usage patterns to **mental health challenges**. This foundational phase ensures that our methodology addresses the most pressing issues currently affecting youth.

## Month 2: Instrument Development and Ethics Approval
During this period, we will design and validate surveys tailored for teenagers, ensuring language is age-appropriate and inclusive. Simultaneously, an Institutional Review Board (IRB) application will be submitted to obtain ethical clearance, prioritizing participant safety and confidentiality. A primary objective is to create a reliable tool that can detect even a single instance of a mental health crisis trigger without causing unnecessary alarm or distress.

## Month 3: Pilot Testing and Recruitment
We will conduct a small-scale pilot study with twenty participants to test the survey instrument's clarity and reliability. Feedback from this group will be used to refine questions before launching the full recruitment drive. Schools and community centers will partner with us to distribute invitation materials, aiming to reach a diverse demographic while maintaining strict adherence to data privacy protocols.

## Month 4: Data Collection – Phase One
This month marks the beginning of the main data collection phase. Participants will complete the baseline survey detailing their current social media habits and emotional states. Researchers will also begin tracking passive metrics where permitted, such as aggregate usage logs provided by parental consent forms. We must ensure that every challenge faced by the respondents during this process is handled with extreme care and professional support.

## Month 5: Ongoing Monitoring and Support
Throughout the second half of the semester, monthly check-ins will occur to gather longitudinal data on any changes in behavior or mood. During these sessions, counselors will be available if a participant discloses significant difficulties. Our protocol mandates immediate referral pathways should any individual face an urgent situation, ensuring that our research does not overlook the severity of real-world **mental health challenges**.

## Month 6: Final Data Gathering and Closure
In the final month, we will collect the follow-up surveys to analyze trends over the semester. Upon completion of interviews, all participants will receive a debriefing summary explaining the study's findings in accessible terms. Additionally, they will be provided with a list of local resources should they need further assistance addressing any remaining concerns.<|im_end|>

## Month 1: Conceptualization and Literature Review
The first month will focus on defining the scope of research and reviewing existing academic literature regarding how digital platforms influence adolescent psychology. We will identify specific indicators of well-being to track and formulate a clear hypothesis linking screen time usage patterns to **mental health challenges**. This foundational phase ensures that our methodology addresses the most pressing issues currently affecting youth.

## Month 2: Instrument Development and Ethics Approval
During this period, we will design and validate surveys tailored for teenagers, ensuring language is age-appropriate and inclusive. Simultaneously, an Institutional Review Board (IRB) application will be submitted to obtain ethical clearance, prioritizing participant safety and confidentiality. A primary objective is to create a reliable tool that can detect even a single instance of a mental health crisis trigger without causing unnecessary alarm or distress.

## Month 3: Pilot Testing and Recruitment
We will conduct a small-scale pilot study with twenty participants to test the survey instrument's clarity and reliability. Feedback from this group will be used to refine questions before launching the full recruitment drive. Schools and community centers will partner with us to distribute invitation materials, aiming to reach a diverse demographic while maintaining strict adherence to data privacy protocols.

## Month 4: Data Collection – Phase One
This month marks the beginning of the main data collection phase. Participants will complete the baseline survey detailing their current social media habits and emotional states. Researchers will also begin tracking passive metrics where permitted, such as aggregate usage logs provided by parental consent forms. We must ensure that every challenge faced by the respondents during this process is handled with extreme care and professional support.

## Month 5: Ongoing Monitoring and Support
Throughout the second half of the semester, monthly check-ins will occur to gather longitudinal data on any changes in behavior or mood. During these sessions, counselors will be available if a participant discloses significant difficulties. Our protocol mandates immediate referral pathways should any individual face an urgent situation, ensuring that our research does not overlook the severity of real-world **mental health challenges**.

## Month 6: Final Data Gathering and Closure
